one_time_description: Correction of errors: firehall construction costs of $12,386 (FY2018) and $539,522 (FY2019) were expensed in error and reclassified to tangible capital assets, restating prior year figures.
economic_notes: Nation has investments in Nlaka'pamux Resources Limited Partnership (17.9%) and Nlaka'pamux Resources Ltd. (16.7%) accounted for by modified equity method. Kahmoose Construction Inc. is fully consolidated. COVID-19 pandemic declared prior to year-end noted as subsequent event with potential future impact on revenues and expenditures.
notes: Prior year (2019) figures are restated due to correction of errors relating to firehall construction costs ($551,908 total) that were expensed in error in FY2018 and FY2019 and should have been capitalized as tangible capital assets. Comparative FY2019 statements were audited by a different auditor. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($1,451,721) + ISC deferred (-$135,285) + BC First Nations Gaming Revenue ($305,761) + FTISS ($260,332) + Province of BC ($195,325) + First Nations Health Authority ($104,760) + ASETS ($28,256) + New Relationship Trust ($26,500) + CMHC ($22,129) + FNESC ($10,746) = $2,269,545; OSR includes taxation, rental, contracting, other income, interest, investment income, and earnings from business entities. No schedule of remuneration disclosed. Current liabilities not separately itemized on face of balance sheet in extracted text.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
